author: Niche Gamer
text:
Spyro: A Realm Beyond announced Publisher Activision and developer Toys for Bob have announced Spyro: A Realm Beyond, an all-new entry in the beloved platformer series. Spyro: A Realm Beyond is in development for Windows PC (Steam, Microsoft Store), Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2, and PS5 with a release set for spring 2027. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus its […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Persona 6 announced After leaks and rumors, Atlus has finally officially announced Persona 6. Persona 6 is in development for Windows PC (Steam, Microsoft Store), Xbox Series X|S, and PlayStation 5. A release date wasn’t announced. Here’s a blurb on the new sequel, plus a teaser trailer: With over 30 million copies sold worldwide, the Persona series has […]

text:
Crazy Taxi: World Tour announced After lots of teases, Sega has announced Crazy Taxi: World Tour, a brand new entry in their beloved taxi racing series. Crazy Taxi: World Tour is in development for Windows PC (Steam, Microsoft Store), Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2, and PS5 with a release set for 2027. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus its reveal trailer: […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Hellblade standalone spinoff Senua announced Publisher Xbox Game Studios and developer Ninja Theory have announced Senua, a new standalone game in their Hellblade universe. Senua is in development for Windows PC (Steam, Microsoft Store), Xbox Series X|S, and PS5 with a release set for sometime in 2027. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus its reveal trailer: A Celtic warrior […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Persona 4 Revival reveals release date and full trailer Atlus has announced a release date for Persona 4 Revival, their new remake of their beloved RPG classic Persona 4 Golden. Persona 4 Revival is set to launch across Windows PC (via Steam and the Microsoft Store), Xbox Series X|S, and PlayStation 5 on February 18th, 2027. Here’s a rundown on the previously announced remake, plus […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Apple Crumble is a new mystery thriller about saving your grandma from a killer Duck Detective developer Happy Broccoli Games has announced Apple Crumble, a fully voiced mystery thriller that mixes family drama, suspense, and plenty of dark humor. It’s your grandmother’s 84th birthday party, and one of the guests is planning to kill her. Your job is to figure out who before the celebration comes to a very unpleasant […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Shot One Fighters shows off combos and artifacts in new trailer Showcased during Day of the Devs at Summer Game Fest, the upcoming title Shot One Fighters, from Red Moon Workshop, combines traditional fighting game mechanics with run-based progression and randomized upgrades. Instead of selecting a fully built fighter from the start, you’ll gradually create your moveset during each run. New attacks, counters, combo tools, and […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Screenbound gets September release date As announced during the Day of the Devs showcase, the upcoming action-adventure platformer Screenbound from Crescent Moon Games is launching on September 10, 2026. The game introduces what the developers call a “5D” platforming experience. In practice, that means you’re exploring a first-person 3D world while simultaneously controlling a retro-style 2D character on a handheld […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
New RPG Ithaca tackles big questions about environmental resistance The Pixel Hunt, the studio behind the acclaimed narrative experience Bury Me, My Love, has revealed more details about its next project called Ithaca. The game follows Penelope, an environmental lawyer who has grown disillusioned with the systems meant to hold major polluters accountable. After joining the Earth Protection Association, she sets off on a […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Prove You’re Human wants you to convince an AI it isn’t human The team behind the acclaimed 1000xRESIST returned to Day of the Devs with a closer look at Prove You’re Human, their first-person narrative adventure that tackles artificial intelligence from a fascinating angle. Rather than asking players to prove they are human, the game flips the concept on its head. Your goal is to convince an […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Lazy River turns a space waterpark into a zombie-filled co-op shooter Revealed during the recent Day of the Devs showcase, Lazy River is a co-op first-person shooter that asks a very important question: what happens when someone pees in a space waterpark? Set aboard Water Station One, an enormous space-based waterpark built around an endless lazy river, the game begins with what should have been a […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
New adventure game Bub uses real paper art pieces to tell its story Revealed during Day of the Devs, Bub is a short narrative adventure from indie duo Paperfrog that tells the story of an artist struggling with a debilitating illness while trying to preserve his memories through art. You step into the shoes of Bub, a New York artist whose creative abilities are slowly slipping away. As […]

text:

Moss: The Forgotten Relic is set to launch for Windows PC (Steam), Xbox Series X|S, Switch, Switch 2, and PS5 on July 15th. text:
Retro action-platformer Duskfade reveals release date and Switch 2 port Publisher Fireshine Games and developer Weird Beluga announced a release date for Duskfade, their 3D action-platformer game. Duskfade is now launching across Windows PC (Steam), Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2, and PlayStation 5 on August 13th. The Switch 2 port was announced today. Alongside news of the release date is that a new playable demo for […]

text:
Forever Skies launches for Xbox in July alongside new update Indie developer Far From Home has announced Forever Skies launches for Xbox consoles next month alongside a new update. Forever Skies is coming to Xbox Series X|S on July 27th alongside a new “The Final Echoes” update the previously released versions on PC (Steam) and PS5. text:

Mistfall Hunter is set to release across Windows PC (Steam), Xbox Series X|S, and PlayStation 5 on July 29th. An open beta is launching on June 14th, too. text:
The Sinking City 2 launches in August Developer Frogwares has finally announced a release date for The Sinking City 2. The Sinking City 2 is launching across Windows PC (Steam, GOG, Epic Games Store), Xbox Series X|S, and PS5 on August 18th.
